name: Šćepan Polje → Piva Canyon drive (via Plužine)
description: The gateway into Montenegro and the lead-up to Durmitor. Šćepan Polje sits on the Tara River at the BiH/ME border — the Tara rafting operators are literally here. After crossing, the route follows Piva Canyon through 56 unlit tunnels (lights mandatory) with the turquoise reservoir below. One of the most dramatic roads in the Balkans.
communityNotes: Tara rafting departs Šćepan Polje (book ahead, ~€60pp, 3-4h on water). The 56 tunnels are genuinely dark — some 2-3km long, no lighting, sections barely one car wide. Lights on, drive slowly, horn on entry. "Absolutely stunning" is the consensus; the contrast between the black tunnels and sudden canyon vistas is unforgettable. Speed cameras on the M18 approach in RS — obey 60km/h limits exactly.
bestTime: Morning crossing to make Piva Lake + Žabljak by afternoon

name: Plužine (Piva Lake)
description: Small town on Piva Lake (reservoir), the turquoise-blue water created by the Mratinje dam. Free swimming in the lake — cold and clear. Quick 30-min stop between the canyon tunnels and Žabljak. Good petrol and services.
communityNotes: "The colour of the water is unreal — that electric turquoise." The dam wall is impressive to see from below. Free parking and access to the lake shore. No entry fee. Worth a 20–30min stop and a swim if the group has the energy after rafting. Town itself has a small supermarket and a kafana.

name: Žabljak
description: Mountain town at 1,456m — the highest-altitude town in the Balkans and gateway to Durmitor National Park. Small but functional: a few kafanas, supermarket, bakeries. Base for Prutaš hike, Black Lake circuit, and canyon viewing platforms. Nights are cool even in June (pack a layer).
communityNotes: "Bring a sleeping bag liner even in summer — nights drop to 8–10°C at altitude." Supermarket stocked for cooking. The Black Lake circuit (3km, easy, 1h) is worth doing the evening you arrive if tired. Stars here are extraordinary — 1,400m+ altitude, no light pollution. Durmitor NP entry €5/person/day. A few good local restaurants on the main drag.
bestTime: 2 nights recommended: Day 8 arrive evening, Day 9 full hike day

name: Durmitor NP — Black Lake (Crno jezero)
description: Hiking in Durmitor National Park around Black Lake and nearby peaks. Planned route: Crno jezero → Veliki Međed (Sjeverni) mountain tour. As a standalone pin it's redundant — the shortlisted summit days start here anyway; kept as the built-in 'we're tired' fallback (easy lake loop, €5 NP + €2 parking).
communityNotes: "The Black Lake circuit takes 1 hour and is worth doing the evening you arrive — flat, beautiful, pine-lined." Circuit is 3km / 60 min, easy for all fitness levels. Swim in the lake (cold, very clear). NP entry fee €5pp/day. The classic photo spot is from the east shore looking toward the Meded massif. "Even after the Prutaš hike the next day, the Black Lake at sunrise is a completely different experience." Do the circuit in the evening of Day 8 arrival; trail is well-marked.

name: Ostrog Monastery
description: Cliffside monastery built directly INTO a 900m vertical rock face — one of the most visited and venerated sites in the entire Balkans. Two churches: Lower (easier access) and Upper (the cave chapel, carved into the cliff). Free entry, donations welcome. Cover shoulders and knees — it's an active Orthodox monastery and dress code is enforced.
communityNotes: Hours: 06:00–17:00 (May–Sep). Go before 09:00 (nearly empty) or at 16:00+ (crowds thinning, still open). Peak summer sees thousands of pilgrims and buses — avoid 09:00–16:00. Road up is narrow/winding; parking fills fast. 'Seeing it built into the rock face is genuinely breathtaking — one of those places that lives up to the hype.' Take the stairs to the Upper church — the Lower is fine but the Upper is the magic. ⚠️ If arriving from Tara rafting (Day 8) → only reach Ostrog ~16:00 — arrive right at window closing. Better on Day 10 with a morning Žabljak start (arrive ~11:00).

name: Skadar Lake (Virpazar)
description: Largest lake in the Balkans straddling the ME/AL border — national park, 264 bird species, endemic carp. Virpazar is the main base for kayak rentals, boat tours, and lakeside winery visits. Turquoise-green water, lotus flowers in June, pelicans.
communityNotes: Best experienced by kayak (Kingfisher Virpazar ~€15-22pp half-day) or wooden boat tour. Lotus flowers bloom June–July. "The lake is extraordinarily beautiful, completely undiscovered compared to Croatian coast." Local wineries (Vinarija Mašanović, Jablan Winery) are a highlight — organic wine, lake views, almost no tourists. No wild camping inside the park boundaries.

name: Sveti Stefan beach
description: Iconic fortified islet with pink-pebble beaches. STATUS for our dates: Aman Sveti Stefan reopens Jul 1, 2026 — three days AFTER we fly out — so the islet itself stays off-limits. The beaches around it are currently FREE (the €120-sunbed era is over; Queen's Beach becomes Aman-exclusive only after reopening), but there are no facilities (no showers/toilets). Reframed: a free swim + the classic roadside photo stop, not an 'iconic beach alternative'.
cost: Beaches free until Jul 1 2026 reopening; no facilities
communityNotes: Aman Sveti Stefan reopens Jul 1, 2026 — three days after the group flies out. So in June 2026 the islet is still Aman-exclusive but the two mainland beaches (Sveti Stefan beach + Miločer/Queen's Beach) are FREE public access with no sunbed charge. "The view from the roadside is iconic regardless — even non-swimmers stop for photos." Mainland beach: fine pebble, beautiful turquoise water, no shade. No facilities while Aman is closed. Good 30-45 minute stop on the Budva→Kotor day. The mainland viewpoint (5min walk from the main road pull-off) gives the classic postcard shot of the islet.
bestTime: Morning swim before heat; afternoon roadside photo stop en route Budva→Bar

name: Budva Old Town
description: Medieval Venetian walled old town on a small rocky peninsula — Montenegro's party capital and most tourist-dense coastal spot. The old town itself is atmospheric with narrow alleys, Orthodox churches, and sea views from the walls. Best known for nightlife (Sparta, Arsenal, various beach clubs) and Mogren Beach, one of the best in Montenegro.
communityNotes: "Budva old town at night is very atmospheric despite the crowds — worth 1-2 hours wandering." June builds fast: Balkan school holidays haven't started yet so the first half of June is better. Mogren Beach (pebble, clear water, 4-5min walk from old town through short tunnel) is the one to go to. "The beach clubs can get obnoxiously loud until 04:00 — if you want sleep, stay 1-2km back from the old town." Parking in old town: near-impossible, use the lot at the top of town.
bestTime: Evening arrival and morning beach swim; avoid midday old town

name: Kotor Old Town + San Giovanni Fortress hike
description: UNESCO-walled Venetian old town at the innermost finger of the Bay of Kotor. San Giovanni fortress hike (~1,200 stone steps) gives the best bay panorama in Montenegro. From Kotor the group returns toward Dubrovnik via Vitaljina crossing.
bestTime: fortress climb early morning or late afternoon (heat)
communityNotes: "Go at 07:30 — by 10:00 you're sharing steps with 3 cruise ships worth of tourists." Fortress entry €8/adult (free before 08:00). Old town itself is free to wander. Best photo: view from fortress looking down over the terracotta rooftops and perfect bay geometry. Cats everywhere — Kotor is famously a cat city. Avoid midday heat in the walls.

name: Komovi mountains (optional)
description: Optional extra if we have time left from Croatia: summit one of the Komovi peaks or do a light hike. Off the main route (east Montenegro), so only if schedule allows.
communityNotes: Off the main route (Kolašin area, +2-3h from the Montenegro loop). "Komovi is wilder and less touristy than Durmitor — if you want the real Montenegro without any crowds, that's it." Kom Vasojevićki summit (2461m) accessible from Eko Katun Štavna. Only fits if the group finds a spare day or wants to skip the Tara–Žabljak corridor. Realistically: marked as optional extension, low probability given the schedule, but extraordinary if timing works out.
